title: Define MeshCore compatibility channel/config secret policy
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-9c4y.1 research found MeshCore CHANNEL_INFO/SET_CHANNEL expose MeshCore channel names and secrets through the local API, while LICHEN native group/OSCORE material must not be leaked or misrepresented through a compatibility shim. Config write commands also need a clear read-only versus persisted-compatibility policy. What needs to be done: define how LICHEN synthesizes MeshCore channel slots, whether compatibility-local secrets are generated/stored, which config writes are accepted, persisted, ignored, or rejected, and how responses avoid claiming MeshCore RF interoperability. Acceptance criteria: policy is recorded with command impacts; native secrets are never exposed; unsupported writes have deterministic MeshCore ERR/OK semantics; downstream mapping/tests consume the policy.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.4 review: MeshCore BLE now has a first product-mode fixed passkey hook via CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHCORE_BLE_PASSKEY and bt_passkey_set(), but production policy still needs provisioned/generated persistent PIN handling, DEVICE_INFO exposure rules, and CMD_SET_DEVICE_PIN storage/update semantics. Replace the static default with board/user provisioning or settings-backed generation when this policy is implemented.
Implementation evidence 2026-07-01: added docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md defining MeshCore compatibility as local app shim only, not RF interoperability. Policy: one synthetic channel slot 0 named Public; GET_CHANNEL(0) returns placeholder with no native secrets; GET_CHANNEL(n>0) ERR_NOT_FOUND; SET_CHANNEL, SET_ADVERT_NAME, SET_DEVICE_PIN, SET_AUTOADD_CONFIG, SET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE, radio/path/raw writes return RESP_ERR + ERR_UNSUPPORTED_CMD until settings-backed compatibility-local stores exist. Native LICHEN OSCORE/link/provisioning/group/private-key material MUST NOT be exposed via CHANNEL_INFO, DEVICE_INFO, PIN, or channel records. Added canonical vectors for SET_ADVERT_NAME, SET_CHANNEL, SET_DEVICE_PIN, and SET_AUTOADD_CONFIG, each expecting 0101 and adapter_test=true; meshcore_adapter generated fixture now consumes 26 deterministic vectors from 32 source vectors. Validation: local 'PYTHONPATH=python/src python3 test/vectors/generate.py' generated 32 MeshCore vectors; local 'cd python && PYTHONPATH=src ../python/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_vectors.py' PASS 78/78. EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y4: direct native_sim meshcore_adapter PASS 15/15; Twister meshcore_adapter PASS 1/1 scenario, 15 cases, 0 warnings. Reviews: Goodall no P0/P1/P2 on policy/security doc; Wegener no P0/P1/P2 on vectors/tests, noted below-P2 that SET_CHANNEL vector proves opcode-level rejection not future payload-shape parsing; Kepler no P0/P1/P2 acceptance blockers, confirmed doc/vector/test coverage and reminded to include new docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md in commit. git diff --check PASS.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore channel/config secret policy recorded in docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md, native secret exposure explicitly forbidden, unsupported writes have deterministic ERR_UNSUPPORTED vectors consumed by native_sim tests, and reviews/validation are clean.
